danger_signals.visual_form: Sprite-based hostile entities in maze environments
danger_signals.meaning: Active threats to player; destruction required for progression
Primary world threat; final confrontation represents restoration of peace
Obstacles containing both dangers (enemies) and opportunities (power-ups)

danger_signals.visual_form: Six distinct environmental areas with thematic visual design
danger_signals.meaning: World-state of catastrophe; each area represents damage from invasion

safety_signals.visual_form: Collectible objects revealed after destroying blocks
safety_signals.meaning: Temporary enhancement to player capability; strategic advantage
Enhanced mobility and combat capability; introduced in this game

safety_signals.visual_form: Empty arena areas after block destruction
safety_signals.meaning: Safe movement zones; tactical positioning advantages

faction_indicators.color_identity: White Bomberman (modern design introduced in this game); Red/Green/Blue bomber variants in multiplayer
faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Bomberman character sprite; bomb weapon visual
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Female and child Bomber skins available as multiplayer character options
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Player spawn positions within each themed area (Jungle, Volcano, Sea, Castle, Tundra)

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Evil Bagular as primary antagonist; Robot Army as nameless hostile force
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Multiple enemy creature types spawned across levels
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Presence across all six areas; final stronghold on artificial comet

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Spirit Pictures as magical power source
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Conceptual presence; represented through fragmented world state and restoration objective

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Five Spirits protect Planet Bomber; unified world geography
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Inhabitants live in peace; no hostile encounters

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Planet Bomber split into five geographical pieces; Spirit Pictures destroyed; six distinct thematic environments represent fragmentation
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Bagular's Robot Army actively occupies territories; creatures attack on sight; player must navigate hostile mazes

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Sequential destruction of enemies and blocks; recovery of Spirit Pictures across levels; thematic environmental variety indicates different corrupted regions
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player systematically clears maze levels; enemies continue spawning as resistance; power-ups become strategic resources

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Artificial comet of Bagular as sixth and final area; boss-tier antagonist visual presentation
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Ultimate battle arena; confrontation with Bagular directly; reassembly of Planet Bomber upon victory

environmental_storytelling.example: Six distinct visual environments (Jammin' Jungle, Vexin' Volcano, Slammin' Sea, Crankin' Castle, Thrashin' Tundra) communicate specific corrupted regions of fragmented Planet Bomber; each area's visual theme suggests different aspects of invasion damage

environmental_storytelling.visual_form: Planet Bomber divided into five pieces plus artificial comet stronghold
environmental_storytelling.example: Geographic separation of playable areas visually represents catastrophic invasion event without explicit dialogue
Creatures distributed throughout maze structures suggest active occupation and ongoing threat; density and type of enemies indicate level progression difficulty
Recovery of Spirit Pictures across levels serves as visual progression indicator; each recovered picture represents world restoration step

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Attack player on sight; require elimination through bomb detonation; respawn or spawn continuously to maintain challenge
Commands Robot Army; occupies final comet arena; serves as ultimate obstacle to world restoration
Navigates mazes; destroys obstacles and enemies; collects power-ups; progresses through areas systematically
Provide rideable mount functionality; enhance player mobility and combat capability within mazes
Hidden within destructible blocks; grant temporary enhancements; encourage exploration and risk-taking within maze structure

notes: Mega Bomberman (Genesis/Mega Drive port of Bomberman '94) employs environment-centric world-state communication rather than explicit narrative exposition. The five-piece planetary fragmentation serves as primary visual metaphor for invasion catastrophe. Thematic area names with alliterative design communicate distinct regions without complex visual differentiation constraints of 16-bit hardware. The introduction of animal Louies and color-coded Bomberman variants adds multiplayer faction distinction. No traditional faction conflict mechanics present; world-state opposition primarily through enemy AI and level progression rather than political simulation.
